quiz_questions.question_text: A car travels around a circular track of radius 50 m at 20 m/s. What is its centripetal acceleration?
An object moves in a circle of radius 2 m with a period of 4 seconds. What is its speed?
A ball on a 0.5 m string swings in a horizontal circle at 3 m/s. What is the centripetal acceleration?
What is the frequency of an object that completes 120 revolutions per minute?
quiz_questions.explanation: f = 120 rev/min ÷ 60 s/min = 2 rev/s = 2 Hz.

quiz_questions.question_text: A particle moves in uniform circular motion. Its speed is 5 m/s and its centripetal acceleration is 10 m/s². What is the radius of the circle?
An object in uniform circular motion doubles its speed while keeping the same radius. By what factor does the centripetal acceleration change?
quiz_questions.explanation: a_c = v²/r. If v doubles, a_c quadruples (factor of 4) since a_c ∝ v².

quiz_questions.question_text: A point on a turntable 0.15 m from the center moves at 0.3 m/s. What is the angular velocity?
A Ferris wheel has a radius of 15 m and rotates once every 30 seconds. What is the speed of a rider on the rim?
In non-uniform circular motion, what are the two components of acceleration?

options.text: Static and kinetic, particularly in the context of changes
Horizontal and vertical when considering all
Centripetal (radial, toward center) and tangential (along the path)
Gravitational and normal under most circumstances involving

quiz_questions.explanation: In non-uniform circular motion, the centripetal component (v²/r, toward center) changes direction, while the tangential component (rα, along path) changes speed.

quiz_questions.question_text: A marble rolls inside a bowl following a circular path of radius 0.3 m at 2 rad/s. What is the centripetal acceleration?
What is uniform circular motion?

options.text: Motion in a circle at increasing speed
Motion along a straight line at constant velocity
Motion in a circle where the speed is constant but the velocity is continuously changing direction

quiz_questions.explanation: In uniform circular motion, the speed is constant but the direction changes continuously, meaning there is always centripetal acceleration directed toward the center.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is centripetal acceleration?

options.text: Acceleration along the direction of motion
Acceleration that increases the speed of circular motion
Acceleration directed toward the center of the circular path: a_c = v²/r = rω²

quiz_questions.explanation: Centripetal acceleration is directed radially inward toward the center of the circular path, changing the velocity's direction without changing its magnitude.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the definition of 'period' in circular motion?

options.text: The angular displacement per unit time
The radius of the circular path
The time for one complete revolution: T = 2π/ω = 2πr/v
The number of revolutions per second

quiz_questions.explanation: The period T is the time to complete one full revolution. It relates to angular velocity by T = 2π/ω and to speed by T = 2πr/v.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the meaning of 'frequency' in circular motion?

options.text: The number of complete revolutions per unit time: f = 1/T, measured in hertz (Hz)
The speed of an object in circular motion

quiz_questions.explanation: Frequency f = 1/T = ω/(2π) is the number of complete cycles per second, measured in hertz (Hz) where 1 Hz = 1 revolution per second.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'non-uniform circular motion' mean?

options.text: Motion that alternates between circular and linear paths
Circular motion where both speed and direction change, producing both centripetal and tangential acceleration
Motion along an irregular curved path

quiz_questions.explanation: Non-uniform circular motion has changing speed along the circle, so both centripetal (a_c = v²/r) and tangential (a_t = rα) acceleration components exist.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the relationship between period and angular velocity?
quiz_questions.explanation: Since one revolution equals 2π radians and takes time T, the angular velocity ω = 2π/T, so T = 2π/ω.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is the direction of velocity in circular motion?

options.text: Tangent to the circular path at each point
Away from the center of the circle
Toward the center of the circle

quiz_questions.explanation: The velocity vector in circular motion is always tangent to the circle, perpendicular to the radius at each point.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does 'radius of curvature' mean?

options.text: The angle subtended by an arc
The straight-line distance between two points on a curve
The radius of the circle that best fits a curved path at a given point
The total length of a curved path

quiz_questions.explanation: The radius of curvature at a point on a curve is the radius of the osculating circle -- the circle that most closely approximates the curve at that point.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is meant by 'centrifugal force'?

options.text: A fictitious force that appears in rotating (non-inertial) reference frames, directed outward from the rotation axis
The reaction force to centripetal force
A real outward force that acts on objects in circular motion

quiz_questions.explanation: Centrifugal force is a pseudo-force that appears only in rotating reference frames. In an inertial frame, there is only the inward centripetal force; there is no real outward force.

quiz_questions.question_text: What does it mean that circular motion involves constant acceleration?

options.text: Both magnitude and direction of acceleration are constant
There is no acceleration in circular motion
The magnitude of acceleration is constant in uniform circular motion, but its direction changes continuously
Acceleration only exists when speed changes

quiz_questions.explanation: In uniform circular motion, |a| = v²/r is constant, but the direction (always toward center) rotates with the object. The acceleration vector is NOT constant.

quiz_questions.question_text: A roller coaster car (600 kg) enters a vertical loop of radius 10 m at the bottom at 20 m/s. What is the normal force on the car at the bottom of the loop? (g = 10 m/s²)
A conical pendulum has a 0.2 kg bob on a 1.5 m string making a 25° angle with the vertical. What is the speed of the bob?
A race car on a banked circular track (radius 200 m, bank angle 30°) with μ_s = 0.5 between tires and track. What is the maximum speed the car can maintain? (g = 10 m/s²)
A centrifuge spins a test tube at 3000 rpm. The bottom of the test tube is 0.15 m from the rotation axis. Express the centripetal acceleration in units of g. (g = 9.8 m/s²)
A child on a swing reaches the highest point of the arc where the swing makes 60° with the vertical. The swing's length is 3 m. What is the child's speed at the lowest point? (g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: Height drop: h = L(1-cosθ) = 3(1-cos60°) = 3(0.5) = 1.5 m. By energy conservation: v = sqrt(2gh) = sqrt(2(10)(1.5)) = sqrt(30) ≈ 5.48 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A stunt pilot flies a vertical loop of radius 500 m. What minimum speed must the plane have at the top of the loop to maintain the circular path? (g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: At the top, gravity provides the centripetal force: mg = mv²/r, v = sqrt(rg) = sqrt(500 × 10) = sqrt(5000) ≈ 70.7 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A car rounds a flat curve of radius 75 m in the rain (μ_s = 0.4). The posted speed limit is 15 m/s. Is this safe? (g = 10 m/s²)

options.text: Yes, the maximum safe speed is 30 m/s
No, friction cannot provide centripetal force in rain
No, the maximum safe speed is only 12 m/s
Yes, the maximum safe speed is about 17.3 m/s, which exceeds 15 m/s

quiz_questions.explanation: v_max = sqrt(μ_s × g × r) = sqrt(0.4 × 10 × 75) = sqrt(300) ≈ 17.3 m/s. Since 15 < 17.3, the speed limit is safe.

quiz_questions.question_text: A satellite in low Earth orbit experiences an acceleration of approximately 9.2 m/s² (slightly less than g at the surface). If the orbit is at altitude 300 km above Earth (R_Earth = 6371 km), what is the orbital speed?
A ball on a string moves in a vertical circle of radius 1 m. What minimum speed at the top prevents the string from going slack? (g = 10 m/s²)
quiz_questions.explanation: At the top with T = 0: mg = mv²/r, v = sqrt(rg) = sqrt(1 × 10) = sqrt(10) ≈ 3.16 m/s.

quiz_questions.question_text: A washing machine spin cycle operates at 1200 rpm with a drum radius of 0.25 m. What force does the drum exert on a 0.5 kg garment during the spin? (g = 10 m/s²)
